summary: Crisp soundboard capture of the first post‑Egypt show, highlighted by a smoking Scarlet > Fire, Estimated > Eyes and World to Give, with occasional uneven playing.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear soundboard quality that lets Jerry’s tone shine, while the second set delivers classic peaks – especially Scarlet > Fire, the Estimated > Eyes sequence and a moving rendition of World to Give. A handful of reviewers note sloppy moments in Scarlet/Fire and Eyes, but overall the performance is remembered as historic and emotionally resonant.

ai_rating.rationale: 19 reviews with an average of 4.3 stars; most comments are enthusiastic about both the music and audio quality, only a few note specific performance flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clean and well‑balanced; occasional muffled sectors or minor dropouts mentioned but do not detract from overall clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is frequently called a ‘monster’ with seamless transitions; first set considered short but solid, and the Egypt slide show adds historic flavor.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is repeatedly praised as crystal‑clear and his solos are described as amazing, though one reviewer calls some later solos sloppy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mostly unmentioned; one critic notes sluggish bass on Scarlet/Fire, otherwise no specific comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's playing gets a nod on Promised Land; Donna’s vocal harmony on "If I Had the World to Give" is highlighted as a pleasant surprise.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum solo includes the memorable banjo‑lesson interlude; overall drumming is seen as solid.
